channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: What Mexico's richest man thinks Trump should do in his second term
item.description: Mexican businessman Carlos Slim, one of the world’s richest people, holds forth once a year on a wide range of topics.
item.dc:creator: María Verza, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former Colombian President Uribe denies bribery and witness tampering charges in historic trial
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Former Colombian President Álvaro Uribe on Monday denied charges of bribery and witness tampering as he made his opening statement in the first criminal trial of a former president in the country’s history.
item.dc:creator: ASTRID SUAREZ and MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US, Russia relations nearing collapse: Kremlin
item.description: Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Sergei Ryabkov said Monday that the U.S.-Russia relations are on the verge of a major breakdown. Despite President Trump claiming that he spoke with Russian President Vladimir Putin over the weekend, the Kremlin has refused to confirm the call.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Hamas postpones upcoming hostage release, risking ceasefire collapse
item.description: Hamas announced Monday it will hold back releasing hostages at the end of the week until further notice, according to a statement by the group reported by multiple media outlets. Hamas, designated as a terrorist organization by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Most nations miss deadline for plans to fight climate change. UN says take your time to do it right
item.description: Nearly 200 nations faced a Monday deadline to file what the United Nations’ climate chief calls “among the most important policy documents governments will produce this century.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ecuador's presidential election goes to runoff between conservative incumbent, leftist lawyer
item.description: Ecuador will choose its next president in a runoff election in April between conservative incumbent Daniel Noboa and leftist lawyer Luisa González. Crime is a major issue for voters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vatican charity says Trump administration's gutting of USAID is 'reckless' and could kill millions
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — The Vatican's charity said Monday that U.S. plans to gut USAID were “reckless” and could kill millions, while Pope Francis’ point man on development urged the Trump administration to remember Christian principles about caring for others.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Video: Rare ‘Black Demon’ fish spotted alive for first time
item.description: The scary-looking fish is usually found in water depths of 1,500 meters, where little to no sunlight reaches.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas says it's delaying next hostage release, claiming ceasefire violations
item.description: Hamas is delaying the next release of Oct. 7 hostages, accusing Israel of violating the terms of their ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Unspent aid worth billions lacks oversight as Trump dismantles USAID, watchdog warns
item.description: USAID has lost almost all ability to track $8.2 billion in unspent humanitarian aid over the Trump administration’s foreign funding freeze and idling of staff, a government watchdog warned Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ecuador's conservative incumbent and a leftist lawyer advance to presidential runoff
item.description: GUAYAQUIL, Ecuador (AP) — Ecuador will choose its next president in a runoff election in April between conservative incumbent Daniel Noboa and leftist lawyer Luisa González.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump: Palestinians wouldn't have right to return to developed Gaza
item.description: President Trump in a new interview said Palestinians would not have the right to return to the Gaza Strip if it is taken over and developed by the United States, as he has proposed. "No, they wouldn't, because they're going to have much better housing.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Romanian President Klaus Iohannis announces resignation after pressure by populists
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romanian President Klaus Iohannis announced his resignation on Monday following mounting pressure from populist opposition groups, two months after a top court annulled a presidential election in the European Union country.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and ANDREEA ALEXANDRU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Kosovo's prime minister looks for allies for new Cabinet after failing to win parliamentary majority
item.description: Kosovo Prime Minister Albin Kurti’s leftist party has won the most seats in the parliamentary election.
item.dc:creator: Llazar Semini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ecuador's conservative incumbent and a leftist lawyer are leading early presidential voting results
item.description: GUAYAQUIL, Ecuador (AP) — Ecuador is likely to choose its next president in a runoff election later this year as early results of a first-round vote Sunday showed a tight difference between conservative President Daniel Noboa and leftist lawyer Luisa González.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Serbian protesters block key bridge, roads to mark 100 days since deadly canopy collapse
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Striking university students in Serbia blocked a bridge in Belgrade as well as roads throughout the Balkan country on Sunday to mark 100 days since the collapse of a concrete canopy at a train station which killed 15 people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rare wildlife spotting caught on camera in Poland
item.description: A wildlife conservation group in Poland was conducting research on beavers when they spotted two rare black wolves on video footage captured in a forest last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rescuers recover 1 body and search for 28 people in a landslide in southwest China
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Emergency teams in China's southwestern Sichuan province raced against time Sunday to locate 28 people after a landslide triggered by rains killed one person and buried homes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Released Thai hostages return to Bangkok after being held for over a year in Gaza
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Five Thai workers released after being held hostage for over a year in Gaza arrived in Bangkok on Sunday. Sarusak Rumnao, 32, Watchara Sriaoun, 33, Sathian Suwannakham, 35, Pongsak Thaenna, 36, and Bannawat Saethao, 27, were freed on Jan. 30 as part of an exchange arrangement.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: War-torn Lebanon forms its first government in over 2 years to end deadlock
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s new prime minister on Saturday formed the country’s first full-fledged government since 2022.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump must not repeat his Kim Jong Un mistake with Iran, security expert warns
item.description: President Trump revealed this week he wants to pursue talks with Iran to end its nuclear program, but one expert says it could be a repeat of when Trump looked to take on North Korea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New report by panel of experts claims killer nurse Lucy Letby is innocent
item.description: Convicted of killing seven babies and attempting to kill seven more, Lucy Letby's conviction is being questioned by a new report that claims she is a victim of a miscarriage of justice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Russian spy ship caught fire off Syria's coast, officials say. Here's audio of its broadcasts
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The man is insistent: Our ship is in difficulty, so keep your distance, he instructs another vessel over the radio. “Warship on your course,” he says. "I am drifting. I’m not under command.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas frees 3 more hostages as part of ceasefire agreement with Israel
item.description: Hamas released three additional hostages on Saturday in exchange for dozens of Palestinian prisoners as part of the ceasefire deal the terror group reached with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazil's socialist president tells citizens not to buy expensive grocery items as food prices rise
item.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva said in a video message posted to social media that citizens should not buy expensive food items to deal with rising food prices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump sanctions on ICC protects US military and government officials, former NSC staffer says
item.description: Amid the International Criminal Court’s issuance of arrest warrants for Israeli leaders for their role in waging a self-defense war against Hamas, President Donald Trump punished the global body, prompting strong reactions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu gifts Trump controversial item that helped turn tide in war against Hezbollah
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu gave President Trump an unusual gift in homage to the controversial pager operation that targeted Hezbollah terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI, scientists unite to decipher old scrolls charred by Vesuvius volcano
item.description: Hundreds of papyrus scrolls were found in the 1750s in the remains of a lavish villa at the Roman town of Herculaneum.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump’s Gaza Strip proposal reverberates
item.description: Aftershocks reverberated Wednesday from President Trump’s declaration the previous day that the United States should take “long-term ownership” of Gaza and that the Palestinians who live there should be moved.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WGN 9

item.title: Arab nations decry Trump proposal for US to take over Gaza
item.description: Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas called for the United Nations to “protect the Palestinian people.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffries amps up call for two-state solution after Trump push to seize Gaza
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Wednesday flatly rejected President Trump’s proposal for the United States to take over the Gaza Strip, suggesting it would undermine ongoing efforts to free Israeli hostages and bring peace to the volatile region.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9

item.title: USPS resumes accepting packages from China, Hong Kong
item.description: USPS says it's working with CBP to implement a collection process for new China tariffs to avoid delivery disruptions.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Saudi Arabia says it won't establish ties with Israel without creation of Palestinian state
item.description: Saudi Arabia said on Wednesday it would not establish ties with Israel without the creation of a Palestinian state.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation
